chunk_summary: This section provides a template for clinicians to counsel caregivers on complementary feeding for children aged 6–24 months, emphasizing assessment and guidance based on WHO, UNICEF, and Indian Pediatrics guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand the key domains of complementary feeding assessment for children aged 6–24 months.
Learn to use a structured counselling prescription template for caregivers.
Familiarize with WHO and UNICEF guidelines on infant and young child feeding practices.
Incorporate evidence-based practices into complementary feeding counselling.
key_facts: Complementary feeding should begin at 6 months of age while continuing breastfeeding.
WHO recommends feeding frequency of 2–3 meals per day for infants aged 6–8 months, increasing to 3–4 meals for children aged 9–24 months.
Energy-dense, nutrient-rich foods should be introduced to meet the growing nutritional needs of children.
Caregivers should practice responsive feeding, encouraging children to eat without force-feeding.
Monitoring growth and development is essential to ensure adequate nutrition during complementary feeding.
reference_quote: Infant and young child feeding counselling: an integrated course — World Health Organization — https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240032828
document_key_topics: Guidelines for feeding frequency and nutrient-rich foods
Monitoring growth and development during complementary feeding
